implementation\n65\t///\n66\t/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n67\t/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n68\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n69\tpub struct BasicParser {\n70\t    config: ParserConfig,\n71\t    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n72\t    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n73\t    /// current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState,\n77\t    /// Current scope depth (number of active scopes in the hierarchy)\n78\t    scope_depth: usize,\n79\t}\n80\t\n81\timpl BasicParser {\n82\t    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n83\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n84\t        Self {\n85\t            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n86\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n87\t            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n88\t            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n89\t            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n90\t        }\n91\t    }\n92\t\n93\t    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n94\t    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n95\t        Self {\n96\t            config,\n97\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n98\t            current_line_type: scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n115\t        }\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    /// Get the current line type being processed\n119\t    pub fn current_line_type(&self) -> self.current_line_type.is_key_bearing()\n126\t    }\n127\t\n128\t    /// Check if current line is indent-only\n129\t    pub fn is_indent_only_line(&self) -> bool {\n130\t        self.current_line_type.is_indent_only()\n131\t    }\n132\t\n133\t    /// Check if current line is empty\n134\t    pub fn is_empty_line(&self) -> bool {\n135\t        self.current_line_type.is_empty()\n136\t    }\n137\t\n138\t    /// Get the current indent transition state\n139\t    pub fn current_transition_state(&self) -> &IndentTransitionState {\n140\t        &self.current_transition_state\n141\t    }\n142\t\n143\t    /// Check if the parser is currently entering a scope\n144\t    pub fn is_entering_scope(&self) -> bool {\n145\t        self.current_transition_state.is_entering_scope()\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    /// Check if the parser is currently exiting a scope\n149\t    pub fn is_exiting_scope(&self) -> bool {\n150\t        self.current_transition_state.is_exiting_scope()\n151\t    }\n152\t\n153\t    /// Check if the parser is at the same scope level\n154\t    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n155\t        self.current_transition_state.is_same_level()\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    /// Get the current scope depth\n159\t    ///\n160\t    /// Returns the number of active scopes in the hierarchy.\n161\t    /// Root scope has depth 1.\n162\t    pub fn scope_depth(&self) -> usize {\n163\t        self.scope_depth\n164\t    }\n165\t\n166\t    /// Check if we're at the root scope (depth == 1)\n167\t    ///\n168\t    /// # Returns\n169\t    ///\n170\t    /// `true` if we're at the root scope, `false` otherwise\n171\t    pub fn is_at_root(&self) -> bool {\n172\t        self.scope_depth == 1\n173\t    }\n174\t\n175\t    /// Check if we're in a nested scope (depth > 1)\n176\t    ///\n177\t    /// # Returns\n178\t    ///\n179\t    /// `true` if we're in a nested scope, `false` if at root\n180\t    pub fn is_in_nested_scope(&self) -> bool {\n181\t        self.scope_depth > 1\n182\t    }\n183\t\n184\t    /// Get a reference to the scope stack\n185\t    pub fn scope_stack(&self) -> &ScopeStack {\n186\t        &self.scope_stack\n187\t    }\n188\t\n189\t    /// Get mutable reference to the scope stack\n190\t    pub fn scope_stack_mut(&mut self) -> &mut ScopeStack {\n191\t        &mut self.scope_stack\n192\t    }\n193\t\n194\t    /// Get the current scope from the stack\n195\t    pub fn current_scope(&self) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n196\t        if self.scope_stack.scopes.is_empty() {\n197\t            None\n198\t        } else {\n199\t            self.scope_stack.scopes.last()\n200\t        }\n201\t    }\n202\t\n203\t    /// Get parent scope at a specific depth\n204\t    ///\n205\t    /// # Arguments\n206\t    ///\n207\t    /// * `depth_offset` - How many levels up to go (1 = immediate parent, 2 = grandparent, etc.)\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// # Returns\n210\t    ///\n211\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a parent exists at that level, `None` otherwise\n212\t    ///\n213\t    /// # Examples\n214\t    ///\n215\t    /// ```\n216\t    /// let parser = BasicParser::new();\n217\t    /// // If in scope \"a.b.c\", depth_offset=1 returns \"b\" scope, depth_offset=2 returns \"a\" scope\n218\t    /// ```\n219\t    pub fn parent_scope(&self, depth_offset: usize) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n220\t        if self.scope_stack.scopes.is_empty() || depth_offset == 0 {\n221\t            None\n222\t        } else if depth_offset >= self.scope_stack.scopes.len() {\n223\t            None\n224\t        } else {\n225\t            self.scope_stack.scopes.get(self.scope_stack.scopes.len() - 1 - depth_offset)\n226\t        }\n227\t    }\n228\t\n229\t    /// Get the immediate parent scope (one level up)\n230\t    pub fn immediate_parent_scope(&self) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n231\t        self.parent_scope(1)\n232\t    }\n233\t\n234\t    /// Get the scope hierarchy path as a string\n235\t    ///\n236\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n237\t    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n238\t    pub fn scope_path(&self) -> String {\n239\t        self.scope_stack.get_scope_path()\n240\t    }\n241\t\n242\t    /// Get all scopes in the hierarchy as a slice\n243\t    pub fn scope_hierarchy(&self) -> &[crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope] {\n244\t        &self.scope_stack.scopes\n245\t    }\n246\t\n247\t    /// Update scope depth to match the current scope stack state\n248\t    ///\n249\t    /// This should be called after operations that modify the scope stack\n250\t    /// to keep the scope_depth field in sync.\n251\t    fn update_scope_depth(&mut self) {\n252\t        self.scope_depth = self.scope_stack.depth();\n253\t    }\n254\t\n255\t    /// Get all indent transitions from parsing\n256\t    ///\n257\t    /// This requires re-parsing to collect transitions. For more efficient\n258\t    /// access, use the scope stack directly during validation.\n259\t    /// Updates parser state with line classifications and transition tracking.\n260\t    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<crate::parsers::yaml::scope::IndentTransition> {\n261\t        let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n262\t\n263\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n264\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n265\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n266\t\n267\t            // Skip document markers and directives\n268\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" || trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n269\t                // Update state to track that we're on an empty line (marker line)\n270\t                self.current_line_type = LineClassification::Empty;\n271\t                continue;\n272\t            }\n273\t\n274\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n275\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n276\t\n277\t            // Track line type in parser state\n278\t            self.current_line_type = line_type;\n279\t\n280\t            // Only track actual indent changes\n281\t            if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n282\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n283\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n284\t\n285\t                // Update transition state to track this indent change\n286\t                let from_indent = scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n287\t                self.current_transition_state.update(from_indent, indent, has_key);\n288\t            }\n289\t        }\n290\t\n291\t        scope_stack.get_indent_transitions().to_vec()\n292\t    }\n293\t\n294\t    /// Get transition counts by type\n295\t    ///\n296\t    /// Returns (enter_scope_count, exit_scope_count, same_level_count)\n297\t    /// Updates parser state with line classifications during counting.\n298\t    pub fn get_transition_counts(&mut self, content: &str) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n299\t        let transitions = self.get_indent_transitions(content);\n300\t\n301\t        let enter = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_enter_scope()).count();\n302\t        let exit = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_exit_scope()).count();\n303\t        let same = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_same_level()).count();\n304\t\n305\t        (enter, exit, same)\n306\t    }\n307\t\n308\t    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n309\t    ///\n310\t    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n311\t    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n312\t    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n313\t    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n314\t        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n315\t\n316\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n317\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n318\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n319\t\n320\t            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n321\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n322\t\n323\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n324\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n325\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n326\t                {\n327\t                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n328\t                }\n329\t                scope_stack.reset();\n330\t                continue;\n331\t            }\n332\t\n333\t            // Skip YAML directives\n334\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n335\t                continue;\n336\t            }\n337\t\n338\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n339\t\n340\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n341\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n342\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n343\t            if indent_changed {\n344\t                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n345\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n346\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n347\t            }\n348\t\n349\t            // Handle blank lines and comments with type-specific logic\n350\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n351\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n352\t            // Comments are also transparent to scope tracking since YAML parsers ignore them.\n353\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n354\t                // Update last_indent tracking so the next line can detect indent changes\n355\t                // but don't trigger any scope transitions\n356\t                if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n357\t                    scope_stack.set_last_indent(indent);\n358\t                }\n359\t                continue;\n360\t            }\n361\t\n362\t            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n363\t            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but may trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n364\t            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n365\t                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n366\t                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n367\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n368\t                }\n369\t                continue;\n370\t            }\n371\t\n372\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n373\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n374\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n375\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n376\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n377\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n378\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n379\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n380\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n381\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n382\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n383\t                                    dup_err.message()\n384\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n385\t                            }\n386\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n387\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n388\t                            {\n389\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n390\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n391\t                            }\n392\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n393\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n394\t                                line_num_1index,\n395\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n396\t                            );\n397\t                        } else {\n398\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n399\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n400\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n401\t                            {\n402\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n403\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n404\t                            }\n405\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n406\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n407\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n408\t                                    dup_err.message()\n409\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n410\t                            }\n411\t                        }\n412\t                    } else {\n413\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n414\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n415\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n416\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n417\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n418\t                        {\n419\t                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n420\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n421\t                        }\n422\t                    }\n423\t                }\n424\t                Ordering::Less => {\n425\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n426\t                    // Check if we're transitioning to a sequence item at same level as parent\n427\t                    // In this case, we should NOT exit the parent scope\n428\t                    let trimmed = line.trim();\n429\t                    let is_sequence_item_at_parent_level = trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") &&\n430\t                        scope_stack.current_indent() == indent + scope_stack.base_indent();\n431\t\n432\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n433\t                    {\n434\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n435\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}', is_sequence_item_at_parent_level={}\",\n436\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path, is_sequence_item_at_parent_level);\n437\t                    }\n438\t\n439\t                    if !is_sequence_item_at_parent_level {\n440\t                        scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n441\t                    }\n442\t\n443\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n444\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n445\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n446\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n447\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n448\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n449\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n450\t                                    dup_err.message()\n451\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n452\t                            }\n453\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n454\t                            {\n455\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n456\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n457\t                            }\n458\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n459\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n460\t                                line_num_1index,\n461\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n462\t                            );\n463\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n464\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n465\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n466\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n467\t                                    dup_err.message()\n468\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n469\t                            }\n470\t                        }\n471\t                    }\n472\t                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n473\t                }\n474\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n475\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n476\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n477\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n478\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n479\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n480\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n481\t                            {\n482\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n483\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n484\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n485\t                            }\n486\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n487\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n488\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n489\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n490\t                                    dup_err.message()\n491\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n492\t                            }\n493\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n494\t                            {\n495\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n496\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n497\t                            }\n498\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n499\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n500\t                                line_num_1index,\n501\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n502\t                            );\n503\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n504\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n505\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n506\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n507\t                                    dup_err.message()\n508\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n509\t                            }\n510\t                        }\n511\t                    }\n512\t                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n513\t                }\n514\t            }\n515\t\n516\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n517\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n518\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n519\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n520\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n521\t                    {\n522\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n523\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n524\t                    }\n525\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n526\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n527\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n528\t                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n529\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n530\t                            dup_err.message()\n531\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n532\t                    }\n533\t                } else {\n534\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n535\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n536\t                    {\n537\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n538\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n539\t                    }\n540\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n541\t                }\n542\t            }\n543\t        }\n544\t\n545\t        duplicate_errors\n546\t    }\n547\t}\n548\t\n549\timpl Default for BasicParser {\n550\t    fn default() -> Self {\n551\t        Self::new()\n552\t    }\n553\t}\n554\t\n555\timpl Parser for BasicParser {\n556\t    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n557\t        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n558\t        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n559\t        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n560\t\n561\t        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n562\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n563\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n564\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n565\t\n566\t            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n567\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n568\t\n569\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n570\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n571\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n572\t                {\n573\t                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n574\t                }\n575\t                scope_stack.reset();\n576\t                continue;\n577\t            }\n578\t\n579\t            // Skip YAML directives\n580\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n581\t                continue;\n582\t            }\n583\t\n584\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n585\t\n586\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n587\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n588\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n589\t            if indent_changed {\n590\t                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n591\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n592\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n593\t            }\n594\t\n595\t            // Handle blank lines and comments with type-specific logic\n596\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n597\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n598\t            // Comments are also transparent to scope tracking since YAML parsers ignore them.\n599\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n600\t                // Update last_indent tracking so the next line can detect indent changes\n601\t                // but don't trigger any scope transitions\n602\t                if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n603\t                    scope_stack.set_last_indent(indent);\n604\t                }\n605\t                continue;\n606\t            }\n607\t\n608\t            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n609\t            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but may trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n610\t            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n611\t                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n612\t                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n613\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n614\t                }\n615\t                continue;\n616\t            }\n617\t\n618\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n619\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n620\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n621\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n622\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n623\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n624\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n625\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n626\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n627\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n628\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n629\t                                    dup_err.message()\n630\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n631\t                            }\n632\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n633\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n634\t                            {\n635\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n636\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n637\t                            }\n638\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n639\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n640\t                                line_num_1index,\n641\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n642\t                            );\n643\t                        } else {\n644\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n645\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n646\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n647\t                            {\n648\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n649\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n650\t                            }\n651\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n652\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n653\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n654\t                                    dup_err.message()\n655\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n656\t                            }\n657\t                        }\n658\t                    } else {\n659\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n660\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n661\t        {\n665\t                            log_debug!(\"[parse_str] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n666\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n667\t                        }\n668\t                    }\n669\t                }\n670\t                Ordering::Less => {\n671\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n672\t                    // Record the indent transition with key\n673\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, true, line);\n674\t\n675\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n676\t                    {\n677\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n678\t             }\n750\t                    }\n751\t                }\n752\t            }\n753\t\n754\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n755\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n756\t                // When starting a new sequence item, exit any existing sequence scope at the same indent\n757\t                if scope_stack.current_indent() == indent && scope_stack.in_sequence_context() {\n758\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n759\t                    {\n760\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Exiting previous sequence scope before entering new one at indent={}, line={}\",\n761\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n762\t                    }\n763\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent.saturating_sub(1));\n764\t                }\n765\t\n766\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n767\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n768\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n769\t                    {\n770\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n771\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n772\t                    }\n773\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent,        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n780\t                    }\n781\t                } else {\n782\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n783\t            // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n797\t                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n798\t                ParseResult::success(value)\n799\t            }\n800\t            Err(err) => {\n801\t                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n802\t                let error_msg = ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n809\t            }\n810\t        }\n811\t    }\n812\t\n813\t    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n814\t        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n815\t        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n816\t            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n817\t            Err(err) => {\n822\t        // Read file content\n823\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n824\t            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n825\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n826\t        }\n827\t    }\n828\t\n829\t    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n830\t        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n831\t        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n832\t            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n833\t        } else {\n834\t            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n835\t        };\n836\t\n837\t        // Run syntax validation\n838\t        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n839\t\n840\t        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n841\t        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n842\t            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n843\t            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n844\t            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n845\t                result.valid = false;\n846\t                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n847\t            }\n848\t        }\n849\t\n850\t        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n851\t        if result.is_valid() {\n852\t            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n853\t            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n854\t\n855\t            // Merge errors from detector\n856\t            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n857\t                result.valid = false;\n858\t                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n859\t            }\n860\t        }\n861\t\n862\t        result\n863\t    }\n864\t\n865\t    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n866\t        // Read file content\n867\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n868\t            Ok(content) => content,\n869\t            Err(err) => {\n870\t                return ValidationResult {\n871\t                    valid: false,\n872\t                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n873\t                        path.display().to_string(),\n874\t                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n875\t                    )],\n876\t                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n877\t                };\n878\t            }\n879\t        };\n880\t\n881\t        // Validate the content\n882\t        self.validate_str(&content)\n883\t    }\n884\t\n885\t    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n886\t        &self.config\n887\t    }\n888\t\n889\t    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n890\t    where\n891\t        Self: Sized,\n892\t    {\n893\t        Self {\n894\t            config,\n895\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n896\t            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n897\t            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n898\t            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n899\t        }\n900\t    }\n901\t}\n902\t\n903\t// ============================================================================\n904\t// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n905\t// assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::EnterScope,\n1712\t                           \"Indent increase should be classified as EnterScope\");\n1713\t            } else if transition.to_indent < transition.from_indent {\n1714\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::ExitScope,\n1715\t                           \"Indent decrease should be classified as ExitScope\");\n1716\t            } else {\n1717\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::SameLevel,\n1718\t                           \"No indent change should be classified as SameLevel\");\n1719\t            }\n1720\t        }\n1721\t    }\n1722\t\n1723\t    /// Test scope operation mapping\n1724\t    #[test]\n1725\t    fn test_scope_operation_mapping()
